headers.comment: Security headers + revalidating cache on EVERY path. CRITICAL: Flutter web's main.dart.js / flutter_bootstrap.js / flutter.js are NOT content-hashed (stable filenames), so caching them 'immutable' froze returning visitors on stale app code for up to a year — that was the recurring 'still broken after deploy' bug. 'no-cache' lets the browser keep a copy but ALWAYS revalidate (cheap 304 when unchanged, fresh 200 when we deploy), so users can never be stuck on an old build.

headers.value: geolocation=(self), accelerometer=(self), magnetometer=(self), gyroscope=(self), microphone=(), camera=(), payment=(), usb=()

default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval' 'unsafe-inline' https://www.gstatic.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: blob:; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'self' https://www.gstatic.com https://fonts.gstatic.com; worker-src 'self' blob:; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'none'; object-src 'none'

headers.comment: Long-lived immutable cache ONLY for genuinely content-fingerprinted asset files (their names change when the bytes change). Large fonts are stable and safe to cache hard.
